agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2025 Annual Municipal Report presented to Council
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and a shorter Highlights version. This report is a yearly summary of the city's finances, services, and progress on goals. It is not a new law or fee, but it gives business owners a snapshot of where the city is heading. Council is set to vote on accepting the report at a future meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
2025 ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT AND ANNUAL MUNICIPAL REPORT HIGHLIGHTS
Purpose: To present the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to replace Development Procedures Bylaw, update fees — effective July 1, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on repealing the current Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw (2022) and replacing it with a new Development Procedures Bylaw (2026), effective July 1, 2026. The proposal also includes amendments to the Burnaby Building Bylaw (2016) and the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. If approved, these changes would update rules and fees for development applications, building permits, and related processes, including new development permit types and formalized fee and refund practices.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if you are planning a development or building project in Burnaby, the new rules and fees could affect your application.

key_dates.label: Proposed effective date — Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw 2026

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT OF BURNABY DEVELOPMENT PROCEDURES BYLAW AND RELATED AMENDMENTS TO THE BURNABY BUILDING BYLAW AND BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW
Purpose:
To seek Council approval to repeal
Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw, 2022
and replace it with the proposed
Development Procedures Bylaw, 2026
, to amend the
Burnaby
Building Bylaw, 2016
and
Burnaby
Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

attachments.title: Proposed Repeal and Replacement of Burnaby Development Procedures Bylaw and Rela

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to apply for UBCM award on development approvals
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider endorsing an application for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards. This is based on the final report for Phase 3 of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program. The DAP program aims to speed up and simplify how the city approves development projects. If endorsed, the application would recognize Burnaby's work on making the approval process faster and more efficient for builders and developers.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed. Watch for the council decision on this endorsement.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
DAP PROGRAM - PHASE 3 FINAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide an update on the activities and outcomes of the Development Approvals Process (DAP) program and seek endorsement to apply for the 2026 UBCM Community Excellence Awards.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for mobile devices and data plans
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services. If approved, this contract would cover the city's mobile communication needs. The decision is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
CA - MOBILE DEVICE AND PLANS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of mobile devices, services, subscription plans, and other data services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on aeration and overseeding contract
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for aeration and overseeding services. This means the city would hire a company to punch small holes in the soil (aeration) and spread grass seed (overseeding) on city-owned fields and parks. If approved, this contract would cover work on sports fields, parks, and other green spaces. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider and vote on the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - AERATION AND OVERSEEDING SERVICES
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for aeration and overseeding services.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract for Victory St. road upgrades from Patterson to Jubilee
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for road upgrades on Victory Street, between Patterson Avenue and Jubilee Avenue. If approved, this project would involve construction work to improve the road. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - (CB-3233) VICTORY ST. - PATTERSON TO JUBILEE ROAD UPGRADES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Victory St. – Patterson to Jubilee Road Upgrades project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on sidewalk contract for five streets
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for new sidewalk construction on Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison streets. If approved, this project would add or replace sidewalks in these areas. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting — it has not been finalized yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - (CA-3299) TRIUMPH, CARLETON, ROSSER, GILMORE, AND MADISON SIDEWALK PACKAGE
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the Triumph, Carleton, Rosser, Gilmore, and Madison Sidewalk Package project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Burnaby's Sidewalk Expansion Program
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for the Sidewalk Expansion Program (project EB-8022). If approved, this change would alter the terms of the contract for building new sidewalks in the city. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - (EB-8022) SIDEWALK EXPANSION PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) to the (EB-8022) Sidewalk Expansion Program.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider comment letters on Metro 2050 land-use amendments for Maple Ridge and Surrey
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider sending comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District about two proposed changes to the regional growth strategy, Metro 2050. One proposal from the City of Maple Ridge would change the land-use designation for an area on North 256 Street. Another proposal from the City of Surrey would change the designation for the Hazelmere area. These are site-specific amendments that could affect future development and land use in those areas. Council will decide whether to support, oppose, or suggest changes to these proposals before they are finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
COMMENT LETTERS ON PROPOSED METRO 2050 AMENDMENTS: CITY OF MAPLE RIDGE (NORTH 256 STREET) and SURREY (HAZELMERE)
Purpose:
To inform Council about two different site-specific Metro 2050 Amendments, one proposed by the City of Maple Ridge and the other by the City of Surrey, and to consider forwarding comment letters to the Metro Vancouver Regional District.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to review street width standards for new developments
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a motion directing city staff to review Burnaby's street width standards for works and services. This means the city would look at how wide streets need to be for new developments, including roads, sidewalks, and utility corridors. A review could lead to changes in the rules developers must follow when building or widening streets. No decision has been made yet — this is just the first step to gather information and possibly propose new standards later.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any future public consultation on proposed street width changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
COTW - PROPOSED WORKS AND SERVICES REQUIREMENTS
Purpose: To direct staff to review the City's street width standards.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on list of consultant budget cuts for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal that would require staff to prepare two separate lists: one showing all consultant budget cuts planned for 2026, including which departments and functions are affected, and another list showing which consultants would keep their funding. This is not a final decision yet — Council will consider the motion at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the lists would help the city decide where to reduce spending on outside consultants next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date — if you work with or rely on city consultants, this could affect contracts or services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - 2026 CONSULTANT FUNDNG AND BUDGET REDUCTIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for staff to provide a comprehensive list of consultant budget cuts, including associated functions and departments, and a separate list of consultants retaining funding for 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan update and direction
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a draft update to the Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan. This proposed plan could change how trails on Burnaby Mountain are managed, including rules for trail use, maintenance, and access. The decision is not final yet — council is being asked for direction on the draft plan at this meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, new trail rules could affect businesses that use or operate near Burnaby Mountain trails.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PRCC - DRAFT BURNABY MOUNTAIN TRAIL MANAGEMENT PLAN
Purpose: To provide an update and seek Council direction on the Draft Burnaby Mountain Trail Management Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider supporting Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid for Bingham Cup 2028
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a request from the Vancouver Roques Rugby club for official support of their bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028. The Bingham Cup is a major international rugby tournament for gay and inclusive teams. Council will consider whether to endorse the bid, which could bring visitors and economic activity to the region. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
PRCC - VANCOUVER ROQUES RUGBY - BINGHAM CUP 2028 SUPPORT REQUEST
Purpose: To seek Council support for Vancouver Roques Rugby's bid to host the Bingham Cup in 2028.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an update on a proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue. Non-market housing means rental units that are priced below the usual market rate, often run by a non-profit or government agency, to make them more affordable for lower-income households. This is an early-stage proposal — Council will consider the update and may decide on next steps, such as public consultation or a formal vote, at a future meeting. No final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
PROPOSED NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECT AT 3961 AND 3983 NORLAND AVENUE
Purpose: To provide an update on the proposed non-market housing project at 3961 and 3983 Norland Avenue.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to review status update on Burnaby civic projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on significant civic projects in Burnaby at an upcoming meeting. This report provides an overview of how major city-building projects are progressing, including timelines, budgets, and any changes. While no final decisions are being made at this stage, the update helps Council track progress and plan next steps. Business owners should be aware that these projects could affect local construction, traffic, and development opportunities in the coming months.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider status update on major engineering projects (Dec 2025–Apr 2026)
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive a status update on major engineering projects planned for December 2025 through April 2026. This is a proposed update — not a final decision — that outlines upcoming work on things like roads, water mains, sewers, and other public infrastructure. The report is meant to keep Council informed so they can decide on next steps, including budgets, timelines, and any changes to the project list. Business owners should watch for this update because it could affect construction schedules, road closures, or utility work near their property.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: MAJOR ENGINEERING PROJECTS - DECEMBER 2025 - APRIL 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on major Engineering Projects.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider status update on IT initiatives for early 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on the city's IT initiatives for January to March 2026. This is a report to keep council informed about ongoing technology projects. No new rules or fees are proposed at this time. The update is for information only and does not require a vote on new bylaws or policies.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
FMC - SU: IT INITIATIVES - JANUARY TO MARCH 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the IT initiatives for January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to review Langara College study on inclusion in public recreation
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College. This project looks at how to make public recreation programs and spaces more inclusive, diverse, equitable, and accessible for Burnaby's diverse communities. The update shares early findings and shows how the work supports better access and participation. No final decision has been made yet — this is a progress report for council's information and discussion.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
PRCC - INCLUSION, DIVERSITY, EQUITY AND ACCESSIBILITY (IDEAS) IN PUBLIC RECREATION RESEARCH IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide an update on the IDEAs in Public Recreation research project, led by Langara College, outlining its purpose, partnership model, and progress to date, while sharing emerging insights and demonstrating how the work supports improved access, inclusion, and participation in public recreation for Burnaby’s diverse communities.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Community Safety and Well-Being Plan update for 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan, which outlines local strategies to improve safety and quality of life. A proposed 2026 update to the plan is also up for discussion. This is not a final decision — Council is set to review the progress report and decide on next steps at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and review the proposed 2026 update — it may affect local safety programs and community services.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
PSC - SU: BURNABY COMMUNITY SAFETY AND WELL-BEING PLAN PROGRESS REPORT AND 2026 UPDATE
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on the 2025 Community Safety and Well-Being Plan.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive update on Burnaby Fire Department operations to February 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a status update on the Burnaby Fire Department's operations through to February 2026. This is a report for information only — no bylaw or policy change is being proposed at this time. The update covers the department's activities, response times, and any operational highlights or challenges. Business owners should be aware that this is a routine update, not a decision that will directly affect their operations.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.7
PSC - SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (FEBRUARY 2026)
Purpose: To provide Council with the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of February 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider RCMP policing update and performance report
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review a status update on RCMP operations covering December 2025 to January 2026, along with the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report for October to December 2025. This is a report for information and discussion — no bylaw or fee change is proposed. The report gives Council a snapshot of recent policing activity and how well the RCMP is meeting its annual goals. Business owners should be aware that this is a routine update, not a new rule or enforcement change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.8
PSC - SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (DECEMBER 2025 - JANUARY 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (OCTOBER - DECEMBER 2025)
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on policing operations covering December 2025 to January 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering October to December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026 to replace 1965 rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a major update to Burnaby's zoning rules. A proposed bylaw, called Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, would completely replace the current 1965 Zoning Bylaw. This includes new Development Permit Area Guidelines (rules for getting permits to build or develop) and a reassignment of zoning districts (which decides what you can build and do on your property). If approved, this would change what types of businesses, buildings, and activities are allowed in different parts of the city. The decision is not final yet — council will consider it at upcoming meetings.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules would affect your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14815 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update penalty rules for zoning violations
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update how the city enforces penalties for breaking the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw. The current penalty rules, which are based on the old 1965 Zoning Bylaw, would be replaced with new rules that match the updated 2026 Zoning Bylaw. This means if you own a business or property in Burnaby and violate zoning rules (like using a building for the wrong purpose or building without a permit), the fines and enforcement process could change. The proposal does not change the zoning rules themselves — it only updates the penalty and enforcement system to match the city's current zoning code.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, this could affect how zoning fines are applied to your property or business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14816 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 4, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (repeal Schedule A, Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 section and replace with Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to amend definitions of 'affordable housing' in ACC and DCC waiver bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the definitions of 'affordable housing' and 'Zoning Bylaw' in the Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges (ACC) and Development Cost Charges (DCC) Bylaw 2024. Amenity Cost Charges are fees developers pay to fund community amenities like parks and community centres. Development Cost Charges are fees for infrastructure like roads and sewers. The proposed changes could affect which housing projects qualify for fee waivers or reductions, potentially impacting development costs and housing affordability in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development project's eligibility for fee waivers.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14817 - Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of Amenity Cost Charges and Development Cost Charges Bylaw 2024, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Waivers and Reductions of ACC and DCC Bylaw 2024 (definitions of "affordable housing" and "Zoning Bylaw")

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026 for redevelopment impacts
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026. This bylaw would create new rules to protect tenants who are forced to move because their building is being redeveloped (torn down or rebuilt). The proposal is still in the early stages — it has received first, second, and third readings, but it has not been finally approved or enacted yet. If passed, it could affect how landlords and developers handle tenant relocation, notice periods, and compensation.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council vote — this bylaw could change how you manage tenant relocations during redevelopment.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.4
#14821 - Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish protections for tenants impacted by redevelopment

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to strengthen tenant protection enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This change would update how the city enforces rules that protect tenants. If approved, it could mean new or stronger penalties for landlords who break tenant protection rules. The goal is to make it easier for the city to hold landlords accountable and give tenants better protection under local law.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how tenant complaints are enforced against landlords.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.5
#14822 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (tenant protection)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update fees and charges mid-year 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the City of Burnaby's Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw with mid-year amendments for 2026. If approved, this would change the fees the city charges for various permits, licences, and services. The exact fee changes are not listed in this agenda item, but business owners should watch for the specific adjustments that could affect their operating costs.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and review the updated fee schedule once published to see how your business costs may change.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.6
#14823 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw (2026 mid-year amendments)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of an amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, the new rules would allow these devices on certain streets and paths under specific conditions. The proposed bylaw is not yet in effect — it is up for a final vote at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the pilot program could affect how you operate a delivery, food service, or retail business that uses e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby parks e-bike and e-scooter pilot bylaw amendment
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters in city parks. If approved, this change would permit these devices in certain park areas on a trial basis. The proposed bylaw is up for approval at the next council meeting. This is not yet law — it is a proposal that council will decide on.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this pilot could affect where you can ride e-bikes and e-scooters in Burnaby parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment to enforce e-bike and scooter pilot
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009. This change is needed to support a new pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, the amendment would allow the city to issue tickets and enforce rules specifically for these electric devices during the pilot. The bylaw notice enforcement system is how the city handles fines for breaking local rules — this amendment would add e-bikes and e-scooters to that system so officers can enforce the pilot's rules.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, this could affect how e-bikes and e-scooters are regulated in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would set the tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in the city for the 2026 tax year. It is based on sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter, which give the city the authority to set property tax rates. If approved, this bylaw will determine how much property tax you will pay next year.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on abandoning rezoning for 3900 Grand Promenade
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to abandon a rezoning bylaw for four properties at 3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place. The original rezoning, approved in September 2024, allowed two market strata residential towers (80 and 73 storeys) plus a 13-storey retail and office podium. The developer now wants to remove the commercial office space and add 292 more market strata residential units instead. Council will decide whether to scrap the old plan and let the developer submit a new one.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, the developer will submit a new rezoning application that could affect nearby property values and development plans.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.3.1
#14737 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 6, 2025 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 9850 Austin Road, and 9858 and 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 and 73 storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. The applicant is proposing to remove commercial office use and replace it with additional market strata residential use, which includes the proposal of 292 additional market strata units
